creator: pkelsey@freebsd.org
text:
I was able to reproduce this on a test VM.  The problem is not that the rm_lock's internal mutex was owned by another thread, but that it was not initialized.  It was not initialized because tcp_fastopen_init() is being called too late in tcp_init().  The bottom half of tcp_init() only executes in the default VNET in order to initialize global TCP resources, so the call to tcp_fastopen_init() there was only working for the default VNET and not for any other.

Alex,

Can you try this following patch on your system?  This resolves it on my test VM.

Index: sys/netinet/tcp_subr.c
===================================================================
--- sys/netinet/tcp_subr.c      (revision 313068)
+++ sys/netinet/tcp_subr.c      (working copy)
@@ -679,6 +679,10 @@ tcp_init(void)
        V_sack_hole_zone = uma_zcreate("sackhole", sizeof(struct sackhole),
            NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, UMA_ALIGN_PTR, 0);

+#ifdef TCP_RFC7413
+       tcp_fastopen_init();
+#endif
+
        /* Skip initialization of globals for non-default instances. */
        if (!IS_DEFAULT_VNET(curvnet))
                return;
@@ -732,10 +736,6 @@ tcp_init(void)
 #ifdef TCPPCAP
        tcp_pcap_init();
 #endif
-
-#ifdef TCP_RFC7413
-       tcp_fastopen_init();
-#endif
 }

 #ifdef VIMAGE
